item.title: Eli Lilly / Foundayo (Obesity Pill)
item.description:
• Summary: Eli Lilly has announced the launch price for its oral obesity drug Foundayo, positioning it competitively against Wegovy (semaglutide) in the growing market for weight management therapies.
  • MoAs: Foundayo is an oral GLP-1 receptor agonist or similar incretin mimetic that enhances insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon release, and promotes satiety to reduce calorie intake and improve metabolic control.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive

item.title: Genetics & GLP-1 Outcomes
item.description:
• Summary: New research indicates that genetic factors may slightly influence patient outcomes with GLP-1 agonist treatments, such as those for obesity and type 2 diabetes. This highlights potential variability in therapeutic efficacy and the role of personalized medicine.
  • MoAs: GLP-1 agonists mimic the glucagon-like peptide-1 hormone to increase insulin secretion, decrease glucagon release, slow gastric emptying, and promote satiety.
  • Sources: STAT News

item.title: Obesity & Ultra-Processed Food Research
item.description:
• Summary: This theme discusses scientific research on the link between ultra-processed foods and obesity, focusing on mechanisms like palatability-driven overeating and its metabolic consequences, with implications for public health strategies.
  • MoAs: N/A
  • Sources: STAT News
